items.original_body:
I updated to the current version, but now I cannot do important changes in the configuration. It seems to be permission issues.

Cann anyone tell, how the permissions to the directories etc. have to look like? Thanks!

![avideo-users-not-permittet](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10559009/105373281-d0f1d180-5c06-11eb-800b-12bfa200f408.PNG)
items.mutated_body: I updated to the current version, but now I cannot do important changes in the configuration because the required permissions are not available.

items.original_body: - [x] add suspend mode and refactor reboot and poweroff\r\n- [x] Set brightness at startup
items.mutated_body: - [x] add suspend mode and refactor reboot and poweroff\r\n- [x] Set brightness at startup

items.original_title: METABOIGNITER: Migrate all docs to JSON parameter schema
items.original_body:
Hi!

this is not necessarily an issue with the pipeline, but in order to streamline the documentation group next week for the hackathon, I'm opening issues in all repositories / pipeline repos that might need this update to switch from parameter docs to auto-generated documentation based on the JSON schema.

This will then supersede any further parameter documentation, thus making things a bit easier :-)

If this doesn't apply (anymore), please close the issue. Otherwise, I'm hoping to have some helping hands on this next week in the documentation team on Slack https://nfcore.slack.com/archives/C01QPMKBYNR
items.mutated_title: METABOIGNITER: Migrate all docs to JSON parameter schema
items.mutated_body: Hi! this is not necessarily an issue with the pipeline, but in order to streamline the documentation group next week for the hackathon, I'm opening issues in all repositories / pipeline repos that might need this update to switch from parameter docs to auto-generated documentation according to the JSON schema. This will then supersede any further parameter documentation, thus making things a bit easier :). If this doesn't apply (anymore), please close the issue. Otherwise, I'm hoping to have some helping hands this next week from the documentation team on Slack https://nfcore.slack.com/archives/C01QPMKBYNR

items.mutated_body: The ZUNION command is missing in the sorted set API https://redis.io/commands/zunion, understant.

items.original_body:
I'm trying to migrate from the old loginapp "objectiflibre/login-app:latest", and cannot get this one here to work. What I am currently getting is

{"level":"fatal","msg":"1 error(s) decoding:

* 'Web.Kubeconfig.ExtraOpts' expected a map, got 'string'","time":"2021-01-02T23:33:48Z"}

in the logs the loginapp Kubernetes pod, same as in the previous issue. I tried to add the suggested snipped to the config, but still the same error.

Any help appreciated, in particular a suggestion for a minimal working example for a config file which can be mounted to /app/config.yaml, using a deployment applied directly with kubectl.


**Loginapp & Dex version**
loginapp: `latest (v3.2.0)`
dex: latest (not relevant yet I think)

**Configuration**

Current loginapp (and eventually dex) configuration (*without secrets*).
I am mounting this in app/config.yaml inside the container (starting the deployment manually, without helm).


```
log:
 level: debug
name: "Kubernetes Authentication"
listen: "https://0.0.0.0:32443"
oidc:
 client:
 id: "loginapp"
 secret: REDACTED
 redirect_url: "https://<server>/callback"
 issuer:
 url: "https://dex.auth/dex"
 rootCA: "/etc/ssl/ca.pem"
 scopes:
 - email
 - groups
 web:
 kubeconfig:
 extraOpts: {}
tls:
 enabled: false
clusters:
 - name: mycluster
 server: https://mycluster:7443
 certificate-authority: |
 -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
 ...
 -----END CERTIFICATE-----
 insecure-skip-tls-verify: false
web:
 mainClientID: "loginapp"
 mainUsernameClaim: "email"
 kubeconfig:
 extraOpts: {}
```


deployment:


```
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
 name: dex-loginapp
 namespace: auth
 labels:
 app: dex-loginapp
spec:
 replicas: 1
 ...
 spec:
 containers:
 - name: dex-loginapp
 #image: objectiflibre/login-app:latest
 image: quay.io/fydrah/loginapp:latest
 command: ["/loginapp"]
 args: ["serve", "/app/config.yaml"]
 volumeMounts:
 - mountPath: /etc/ssl/ca.pem
 name: dex-ca
 readOnly: true
 - mountPath: /app/config.yaml
 name: loginapp-config
 readOnly: true
 volumes:
 - hostPath:
 path: /cephfs/certs/dex/ssl/ca.pem
 type: File
 name: dex-ca
 - hostPath:
 path: /cephfs/volumes/dex-loginapp/config.yaml
 type: File
 name: loginapp-config
```

I tried to add the suggested snipped to the config, but still the same error. Any help appreciated, in particular a suggestion for a minimal working example for a config file which can be mounted to /app/config.yaml, using a deployment applied directly with kubectl. **Loginapp & Dex version** loginapp: `latest (v3.2.0)` dex: latest (not relevant yet I think) **Configuration** Current loginapp (and eventually dex) configuration (*without secrets*). I am mounting this in app/config.yaml inside the container (starting the deployment manually, without helm). ``` log: level: debug name: "Kubernetes Authentication" listen: "https://0.0.0.0:32443" oidc: client: id: "loginapp" secret: REDACTED redirect_url: "https://<server>/callback" issuer: url: "https://dex.auth/dex" rootCA: "/etc/ssl/ca.pem" scopes: - email - groups web: : extraOpts: {} tls: enabled: false clusters: - name: mycluster server: https://mycluster:7443 certificate-authority: | -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- ... -----END CERTIFICATE----- insecure-skip-tls-verify: false web: mainClientID: "loginapp" mainUsernameClaim: "email" : extraOpts: {} ``` deployment: ``` apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: dex-loginapp namespace: auth labels: app: dex-loginapp spec: replicas: 1 ... spec: containers: - name: dex-loginapp #image: objectiflibre/login-app:latest image: quay.io/fydrah/loginapp:latest command: ["/loginapp"] args: ["serve", "/app/config.yaml"] volumeMounts: - mountPath: /etc/ssl/ca.pem name: dex-ca readOnly: true - mountPath: /app/config.yaml name: loginapp-config readOnly: true volumes: - hostPath: path: /cephfs/certs/dex/ssl/ca.pem type: File name: dex-ca - hostPath: path: /cephfs/volumes/dex-loginapp/config.yaml type: File name: loginapp-config ```

items.original_title: [Fix Flaps] When flaps 0 aircraft shouldn't be able to take off
items.original_body:
<!-- ⚠⚠ Do not delete this issue template! ⚠⚠ -->
<!-- Issues that do not use the issue template are likely to be ignored and closed. -->


**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...] -->


**Describe the feature you'd like to see implemented**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->
When using flaps 0 aircraft shouldn't be able to take off. The warning signs work fine but even so using flaps in 0 position shouldn't be able to take off.

items.original_body: If transaction processing is timing out: the nodeos setting that will likely fix that is max-transaction-time = 100 or add --max-transaction-time 100 to startup command
items.mutated_body: transaction processing is timing out: the nodeos setting that will likely fix that is max-transaction-time = 100 or add --max-transaction-time 100 to startup command

items.original_body: When I open the app, it immediately crashes with a TypeError. Expected behavior: the app should show the home screen.
items.mutated_body: When I open the application, it immediately crashes with a TypeError. Expected behavior: the home screen should be shown.
